channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. — 198 replies — 792.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job.

Open thread • Replies: 198 • Updated: 2026-07-13 15:29 UTC

OP

I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. I'm not young, 40 next year, and don't have a job history or references. High school graduate.
How fucked am I?

Early replies (first 3)

62449979
I live in California, too.

62449982
What did you do for the last 23 years? Hard to help without more context. Do you have skills?

62449989
>>62449982
Depression and NEET means not in education employment or training.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62472133
I always thought I was a fucking loser.... And then I run into a thread like this. Thank you for making me feel better about myself. Holy shit, is this the end boss of man child or what? 40? Fucking 40 and you argue with your mom about your internet connection? I'm sorry, I mean HER internet. You might not want to conform to the rules of society like some retarded hippie, but even the hippie found a way to make money.

Seriously KYS. That's the best advice you can get here, you fucking baby. Want some sympathy and fake advice? Go cry on Reddit.

62472153
>>62449961
Work in medical field, work grunt white collar job and be a yes man, do a niche trade or work 2 brain dead easy low paying jobs and basically collect money for existing. Many jobs like that in California, btw nothing is beneath you and you're fucking lazy, stop making threads here and go do something, but you're trolling anyways. You only get to your spot in life because your insane ego fooled you into not doing anything

62473146
>>62468936
If you have a master's there should already be some bs job that hires you.
I know 2 people in your shoes and they did exactly that, some bs gov job or companies who contract with military.
The Master degree tells them you're not retarded and can learn the job but has no relevance to your schooling

62473155
>>62449961
I bet you were one of those neet posters and now mommy and daddy are getting to old or are dead/dying and you suddenly have to get that wagie job you were making fun of

62473189
>>62473155
>t. wagie
Kek. I am neet and also 40

62473197
>>62473155
Seething wagie, i live toyally of NEETbux + crypto. Tick tock, wagefuck.

62473199
>>62473155
Being a slave isn't a good thing. You're supposed to get out of a bad situation not build a personality around it and defend like a vegan feminist.

62473272
>>62473197
You the VA guy?

62473749
>>62473272
It’s the VA guy

62473786
Well, nevermind that shit. I just went to that store and saw who they hired instead of me. I don't know why I bothered.

62473888
It's the same shit as before, nobody even looks at my applications, nobody even talks to me.

62474038
>>62473888
you decided to look for jobs at the worst time, you retarded nigger

62474334
What are you supposed to do? Nothing you're told to do works.

62474529
>>62449961
just go sell cars. just go into sales. doesnt matter what

62474626
>>62451403
im in the same boat as you OP. start using your local resources. you said you live in the woods? start selling firewood. grow a bunch of plants and start a nursery and start selling plants. designer plants for decoration like japanese maples and shit. its a good side gig. make a website and shit for it then in a year or two when you apply for a real job say you were an entreprenuer and sold your business and want the security net of a regular 9-5 job now with the shitty economy. you can also just backdate this shit and fabricate a website and reviews for something that never happened too btw if you want to just start applying now withou getting any extra money or learning how business works

62474732
>>62450440
They don’t hire anyone retard

62474735
>>62452288
They
Don’t
Hire
You

62474751
even though I'm not a neet after being laid off I feel like I'm in a similar situation.
I have a leg up with my work experience and education but I don't feel like it was worth it which meant that I wasted my youth doing something even worse than just fucking around

62474795
to all the people in this situation who want actual sane advice, here it:

Do something easy. Something that won't demand a superior work ethic or a miserable grind with a manager looking at production numbers watching you.
You're likely gonna hate your life every single morning you wake up and have to work AND you didn't do this when you were 20 years old and it was easy. You can get over the hump and become a "worker" but the mornings are already your enemy, don't stack the odds further against you.

Work behind a register. Ideally at a gas station or some sleepy business. Maybe a long shot for you to get hired, but it's ideal. Some cleaning, some dealing with dickheads, some stocking. You won't be worked to the bone. A grocery/dollar store cashier is worse in most cases.

Inbound call centre. Yes you will hate your job but the work comes at you and your office should be air conditioned. If you have superior (compared to your coworkers) manners and people skills you may even find yourself promoted rapidly. 60 year old grannies get jobs in call centers and end up trainers and managers, you can do it.

Janitor. This one may be a stretch for a 40yo NEET but if you get the right gig, or hop into the right gig after getting a year experience, you may have found a cozy job. Many janitors are not running around like rats and barely have to deal with coworkers or the public. Maybe you end up hired by the local school board in half a year and make "decent" money.


If you really want to try your hand at job where you have a taskmaster watching you try these:

Warehouse worker.
Meatpacker.
Day laborer: Construction, landscaping, whatever.
Factory as a production worker. GL getting hired unless the job is literal hell with high turnover.
Outbound Call Centre

Good luck. If you show up every day for a 1 year or 2 a manager or senior coworker will likely give you a glowing reference. You can even become a real "worker" and have easier mornings in time.

62474821
>>62474795
None of those are hiring.

62474824
>>62474821
Then keep whining here like a bitch. Someone else will take the advice and make a few dollars. If a call centre won't take you, you may as well start picking up cans and bottles.

62474838
>>62474824
Just admit that you're wrong. There are no jobs.

62474844
>>62474795
if you're a man you don't exist. don't go to die in a dead end gas station. karma is real.

62474884
>>62449961
Anon, try asking an LLM (I recommend chat gpt but use whatever) on how to get a job. Explain your entire situation in as much detail as possible, why you haven't worked, depression, you're 40, never had a job, where you live (so it can recommend options) that you're carless.

Explain EVERYTHING. Learning how to utilize LLMs will really help you anyway. I'm getting close to 40 myself and I use LLMs daily (I am a professional with a good job not a NEET but it doesn't matter you need to use LLMs now even if you're employed). Anons ITT are mostly going to dunk on you because you're a huge fucking loser but the best time to turn your life around is right the fuck now. The LLM won't make you feel bad like everyone here is doing too.

Explain your interests to it too and maybe it can try and recommend a job you'll actually excel somewhat in, although beggars can't be choosy at this point.

62474892
>>62474884
the creature making the OP does not want advice. It's doing an act for (you)s
This is actually good advice to someone so defeated they can't find jobs felons on the sex offender registry get after 15 years binned up.
I'm sure a free AI could coach a defeated NEET into one of those jobs.
OP isn't though, just a weird fuck

62475051
>>62474884
I think this is the first time I've seen someone call Ai like chat gpt an "LLM". Well, I guess I've got nothing better to do.

>>62474892
Get a life you fucking loser. Dogshit like you doesn't deserve even the attention I've given you already.

62475932
>>62475051
>Well, I guess I've got nothing better to do.
Repeating myself, but the modern human needs to get comfortable using LLMs to be productive and successful. Avoid falling in to emotional or political traps involving them. View them as a useful tool. A human who refuses to use a calculator would be less productive than a human who does use a calculator.

The same is true for LLMs. Don't blindly trust everything it tells you. Get a feel for how to prompt it to give you the results you want and double check things that you aren't sure about. A lot of people on /biz/ and in general seethe over LLMs, call you a pajeet if you mention them etc, just ignore them. I'm a professional making 6 figs a year in an white collar position, I use LLMs every single day outside of work and inside of work.

As a skill-less NEET who doesn't know how to navigate the world it's the most invaluable tool at your disposal.

62475990
>>62449961
very white european of you

62476030
>>62475932
You put way too much faith in an algorithm poorly masquerading as ai. If it's a tool it should be a learning tool, not a crutch.

62476048
>>62476030
Nowhere did I say it's a crutch, I very clearly said it's a tool. You're starting to sound like you're beyond helping desu. Good luck.

item.title: BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan? — 145 replies — 147.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan?

Open thread • Replies: 145 • Updated: 2026-07-13 14:33 UTC

OP

BTC is down again, but are you buying the dip or waiting for lower prices?

>Do you think this is just a normal correction, or is there more downside ahead? What's your strategy?

Early replies (first 3)

62426518
I've been shorting this garbage all month

62426527
>>62426518
Wow, you've got good profit by now then?

62426535
>>62426527
up 80% on some 2x short BTC etf

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62455432
>>62454976
Ok

62455827
>>62426535
How you doing now, bobo?

62459212
>>62455827
Lmao

62459912
>>62444587
I like the idea behind their TBV, as it helps to keep all transactions on the BTC chain

62463121
>>62450912
Depends on you

62464112
>>62459912

62466089
>>62450895
ofcourze.. keep accumulating

62466111
Epstein pedo coin radioactive garbage

62466317
I'm just sticking to DCA instead of trying to catch the exact bottom. Every cycle has these pullbacks and guessing the lowest entry usually ends up costing more than just buying consistently. I'm curious how Candora's Liquidity Mirror will show how liquidity changes around key price levels once it launches.

62466320
Just get it through your head that it’s down until Trump is out bare minimum and then it entirely depends on whatever degree of a travesty the next administration is. Trump’s presidency is a bust at this point though. He’s going to fuck shit up and run insider trading scams on the market until the party is over.

62466322
I'd rather wait until BTC shows some actual strength before adding more. Catching falling knives sounds good until it doesn't. Candora's Anti-Spoof feature is expected to help distinguish more consistent liquidity from orders that disappear quickly once it launches.

62466325
Depends on what's driving the move. If it's just leverage getting flushed then I'm not too worried, but if macro conditions keep getting worse I'd expect more downside first. I'm interested in how Candora Agent will help analyze market events alongside price action once it launches.

62466346
I'm buying small but keeping plenty of cash on the side. Nobody knows where the bottom is, so position sizing matters more than making the perfect call. Candora's Slippage Protection will help reduce the impact of unexpected execution price changes once it launches.

62466355
The more everyone starts calling for a crash, the more I start looking for buying opportunities. Fear usually creates better entries than hype. I'm curious how Candora's Order Shield will briefly evaluate sudden price spikes before triggering stop orders once it launches.

62467277
>>62466346
>>62466355
Are you okay?

62467279
>>62426512
BUY the freaking DIP!!!!!!

62468736
>>62467279
fuck buy

62469657
Ngmi

62471073
>>62426537
BTC average buy price is 53k. It grows to 76k if you only count active traders and ignore ancient wallets of dead people that no longer move. Sovereign states use it to trade and circumvent embargos. BTC isn't going anywhere unless a flippening happens which I highly doubt it would in the next decade.

62471097
>>62471073

@all, hold your horses. the everything bubble bursts and big correction ahead. we can get coins, stonks and PMs at discount levels at end of the year.

62471732
>>62471097
Buy the dip

62472321
>>62426518
I'm sure you got liquidated now that it has started pumping, the bull is fucking back. I DCA already, also trend around BTC lending and staking keeps increasing, most especially with the likes of TBV getting integrated into AAVE

62472586
>>62426512
People really fail to grasp the significance of project 2025.

The whole thing was class warfare designed to enslave the middle class and transform it to a permanent underclass for the wealthy.

Part of the plan is to get rid of the federal reserve's dual mandate of price stability and high employment and just replace it with price stability. The era of pumping shit up just to pump it is over because the rich guys who actually control shit have decided they would rather have poor people suffer than make more money.

They want economic pain, particularly in the labor markets because they think workers have gotten too uppity. BTC will dump, everything will dump. You aren't gonna be saved by the money printer going brrrr this time.

62472665
>>62472586
Fuck

62472667
>>62426512
I bought and if it goes lower I buy more.

62473416
>>62472667
No way

62474880
>>62471097
Hmm I wonder what the appropriate correction is for a rigged alt currency that's valued at over 62,000 times the primary currency.

62474947
>>62426552
What about gold and silver? Are we expecting it to follow crypto or does it have another cycle?

62475208
>>62474947
Are you ok?

62475830
>>62426512
If Bitcoin keep getting down and statibilize at a sane price it may finally be useful for transaction.

Oh wait you bought at the top because you were speculating?
Rofl, lmao even

item.title: Any advice for going to college for Accounting at the ripe age of 26? — 29 replies — 116.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Any advice for going to college for Accounting at the ripe age of 26?

Open thread • Replies: 29 • Updated: 2026-07-13 15:25 UTC

OP

Any advice for going to college for Accounting at the ripe age of 26? I used to be a NEET and then a wagie at a supermarket.

Early replies (first 3)

62475219
>>62475211
Dnr but would pump many a white baby out of that semen demen wemen

62475227
>>62475219
Only a BBC like mine could satisfy an alabaster beauty of this caliber.

62475234
College is great for hot chick, tinder is a gooner dump site.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62475248
>>62475211
I heard that if you are in the US you can get into some accounting positions
with just a cpa loicense which you get through some expedited programs but Im not sure.
Look up some Clarey's vids he has a hard on for accounting and has always recommended it.

If you can get in without a degree than you should do that, you can get a degree on the side
through online programs while you are actually working.

If you have to get a degree than again - study online, don't waste time and money going to a brick and mortar
place it aint worth it.

62475259
>>62475211
i went back to school for accounting at 27. it's not a bad job. try to decide ahead of time what type of accounting you want to do: tax or anything else.

tax: if you want to work for yourself, go to a small to medium sized public accounting firm. i wouldn't bother with big 4 or large firms, because you won't be dealing with things that concern you as a future tax preparer for small businesses and individuals.

anything else: try to break into a large public accounting firm. you'll have the most opportunities and the best exit opportunities.

i went big 4 audit very briefly, spent the last 6 years in corporate accounting. kind of wish i had done tax (or law school), but it's not a bad job, like i said.

62475285
>>62475259
Yeah it’s definitely not a bad job. Decent pay. Reasonably soul crushing. Can be fairly stressful as you get higher up. When I was a staff accountant or a financial analyst it was so fucking easy and chill, best job I ever had in my life. Then I took a manager job and I’m stressed out all the time. Wish I never took the job. But I need more money as I have kids now.

>t. 15 years an accountantfag

62475301
>>62475211
You have no urge to marry, you can do it if you go back to college.

62475306
>>62475211
Seems like AI can replace accountants but the real job of an accountant is providing cover for business owners and rich people avoiding and evading taxes so you need humans for that.

62475315
>>62475306
People said the same shit when computers were invented, instead it made even more accounting jobs. The eternal accountant will never die.

62475380
You’re not even old and you’re overthinking it. You're going to have a huge advantage over these retarded little 18 year old Gen Alpha dudes when it comes to getting pussy. You’re more developed and you have more life experience, hoes like that shit. Start lifting hard if you aren’t already.

62475382
I remember being 18 in college and talking to a guy who went to the military before going to college and me and all of my friends were making fun of him for being 27 haha

62475396
>>62475382
The avg grad student is in his early 30s now. Kids are going to school later then in the past.

62475410
>>62475211
Claude fable will decimate accounting job market. Retard

62475424
>>62475410
What’s the safest major in the age of AI sloppa?

62475427
>>62475410
i'm a senior manager at a fortune 10 company. we recently had an AI demo week, showcasing claude and other tools. they all sucked and were a complete joke. what do you do for a living?

62475451
>>62475211
At this point pick up trades and become an electrician. Decent pay and assured source of income for decades.
>>62475427
This sounds like the people that laughted at genAI back in 2023 because it didnt know how to draw hands. It's shortsighted, and while it may very well plateau, just the possibility of it improving exponentially warrants some concerns, specially considering OP is deciding his professional trajectory for the next 35-40 years or so.

62475468
>>62475451
you're assuming that LLMs will overcome their inherent limitations concerning hallucinations or numerical calculation ability.

if all white collar work is at risk of LLM destruction (it's not), then why would anyone pursue a career as an electrician? who would be paying for your services as an electrician?

62475494
>>62475211
Learn the phrase "the combo or just the sandwich?"
Remember that no one asks "would you like fries with that?" ever since the combo meal deal was introduced.

Between accounting databases and AI, expect to be saying that phrase a lot after you graduate.

62475507
>>62475468
I am not assuming anything, I'm just saying that the POSSIBILITY of that technology being able to overcome such limits or at least improving itself enough to make OP field obsolete warrants him considering his options, not straight up discarding accounting.
About the electrician, it's a personal suggestion. Everything runs on electricity, houses will always need repairs, installations and maintenance of that kind, for example. If I could go back in time I would have picked that trade.

62475605
>>62475211
Wow she's cute, what an angle. nw needed to coordinate a careful approach towards her?

62475644
>>62475427
>>62475451
The thing about AI is it is useful, but even in its current iteration it has fundamental flaws that mean for specific jobs it can never do as good as an experienced human. Inexperienced people using AI will do better than if they didn't in many cases, but anybody with decent experience sees the output and sees all of the things wrong with it.
As someone put it, people think AI makes good charts or slides or reports, and then the people who are really good at making charts or slides or reports all say "No that looks bad".

I'm a software engineer and I use a decent amount of AI and had it do great for me, but there's still a number of problems I've thrown at it and it produced total unworkable trash. The thing is, if I was inexperienced I would think it looked good. As someone with 15 years of experience though, I could see its solution would break down rapidly with any other alterations we needed to make.
The experienced human in the loop is still required for anything beyond the absolute basic. As someone who is also in the financial space, AI is absolutely not going to be capable of keeping all the ducks of accounting and finance laws in a row versus an experienced human being.

62475661
>>62475644
best case scenario is AI performs like a retarded offshore indian "senior analyst" who actually performs at the level of a shitty onshore american staff accountant. the offshore indian costs $15k a year and, despite all of his defects, once you learn his personality you can kind of trust him, within limits. the AI will always be prone to confidently lying, it has no permanence or personality so you can never trust it, and the token spend on recreating an offshore indian IQ is $20k a year. so what's the point?

62475708
>>62475211
26 not that old. Older sure, but youre on the edge of being fine.
Just be cool. In all situations people will want to associate with you if you improve their life experience.
Also be serious & career oriented. Youre on the tail end of when fucking around is acceptable.

62475719
>>62475424
Healthcare and accounting

62475722
>>62475211

YOU

JUST

KNOW

62475737
>>62475719
the media is talking about the trades now
in about 10 years it'll be this

so you literally have the next 2-3 years to get your foot in the door in one of these fields where you can still last long enough to have a fulfilling career

62475745
>>62475722
It's almost guaranteed in the year 2026

62475849
>>62475719
>>62475737
Won't demand for healthcare decrease as the baby boomers die off? So if you're 5-10 years away from getting into medicine, you'll most likely enter the job market just as demand starts to take a heavy dip

62476033
>>62475849
we're not going to see a population decline and there is still a huge demand now for all healthcare roles. maybe less geriatric care and more plastics and specialist groups

item.title: YABBA DABBA DOO — 29 replies — 116.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
YABBA DABBA DOO

Open thread • Replies: 29 • Updated: 2026-07-13 15:23 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62473718
>>62473608
Can someone with a brain explain this?

62473723
>>62473608
Diamond hand bros....

62473726
>>62473718
Lots bagholders
Many seller overhead
Stock go up bagholder sell
Stock struggle go up

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62473734
>>62473718
It was absurdly overpriced relative to the actual financial business metrics of the company, only retards bought and held. Retail is slowly figuring it out.

62473770
>>62473723
My dick is pretty diamonds right now ngl

62473818
>>62473718
12 hours from now you back up the truck and buy as many shares as humanly possible

62473895
>>62473718
kikes are shaking paper redditors out of the tree.
spacex is still doing better than average for tech IPOs despite leftist lunacy and their irrational hatred for elon musk. niggers who bought at the top got no one to blame but themselves. all IPOs do this.

62473903
IPO Price was $130 so it’s still up

62474110
>>62473608
reminder this thing was originally only 4% float but now half of those shares are locked up through indice buys
it could easily squeeze upwards

62474180
>>62473895
cope

62474335
>>62474110
you have like two weeks for that until they will dump hard. August spcx will be around 80.

62474347
>>62473818
Ah yes the famous IPO snipe! IPO stocks rarely go into an extended downtrend B-)

62474571
>>62473608
FOUR PERCENT :D

62474653
>>62473770
Hardcore sex with Betty.

62475709
SPACEFUCK IS GOING DIRECTLY TO ZERO

62475730
>>62473608
TO ZERO, ASYMPTOTICALLY !

62475740
>>62473895
Genuine cultist chud tard shows you how to lose money RIGHT HERE!

62475750
Didn’t Elon shill that really bad Chud Punisher movie about European Immigrants? Kind of ironic as a South African getting hands out from the US Government.

62475788
I'll put it in crypto terms:
The tokenomics of this thing are worse than vast majority of shitcoins.
From August to the end of the year there are going to be multiple massive token unlocks. By the end of the year the circulating supply will be 4-5x bigger than it is now. The first unlock in early August alone will more than double the current circulating supply.
And most of these unlocks go to people who have held on to locked tokens for years now. They will want to sell, maybe not everything, but every single person will sell at least some.

Oh and China landing reusable rockets now too.

62475805
>>62475750
horse arse

62475808
>>62475805
I bet you into bestiality tho

62475834
>>62473718
>Can someone with a brain explain this?
Grok is, in fact, not worth $26.5 trillion.

62475846
Lol that trash stock has only one way to go and it isn't up

62475863
>>62473608
>Thanks for the trillion, bitch peasants
>remember, immigrants, brown, black, red, yellow are your enemy

62475946
>>62475834
N-no YUGE GIANT PLANET-FOOKEN-SIZE TAM after all, you be saying?

62476016
>>62475730
maybe the faggots who acted smart for buying this shit will learn something

62476017
>>62473718
SpaceX was hugely overvalued by market makers in its IPO so people could write funny news articles about trillionaires, and now price discovery is beginning to drag its valuation down to the correct level

62476019
>>62475834
b-b-b-but SpaceX is a s-s-s-space company!!?

62476023
>>62476019
nigga all the money is on Earth, ayys can't sign up on Robinhood

item.title: Swift is rolling out its own blockchain right now in live production — 92 replies — 114.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Swift is rolling out its own blockchain right now in live production

Open thread • Replies: 92 • Updated: 2026-07-13 14:43 UTC

OP

DTCC is going live on-chain later this year.
Yet crypto is in the shitter, why?

Early replies (first 3)

62463146
>>62463144
Explain how it's bullish for crypto?

62463270
>>62463146
Both Swift and DTCC are going to be using a variety of public chains.

62463273
So SWIFT has their own singular chain. I thought it'd be that each bank has their own chain, connected to each other by CCIP. If it's all done on a single chain then it obviates the need for a lot of CCIP transactions, right?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62465459
>>62463144
>>62463348
everything to do with chainlink

62465696
>>62463428
It isn't though.

62465711
>>62463144
>kek, thanks for the free case study and stress-testing on blockchain networks, suckers!

62465827
Testimg

62465841
>>62463144
>Swift is rolling out its own blockchain
it isn't
a private chain is worthless/ meaningless

62465848
>>62463146
mood! It was supposed to be XRP but they just cucked ceypto and made their own token.

62465917
>>62463144

ICP and DOT

https://youtu.be/W2A53FoHnjs

62466023
>>62465065
I held 2500+ at one point and also went through a belief phase. I remember religiously playing the link rap song, genuinely catchy. Eric Schmidt and sergey fireside was the peak of my hopium and I remember saying to myself this was it, this had to have been it, I mean the CEO of Google in its heyday giving props to the dev of my bags, it felt better than 2016 Trump winning. Every link marine on /biz/ was hyping each other up. Holy fuck Eric Schmidt just told sergey he was a genius and this was the next industrial revolution. Smart contract assassins holy FUCK!

Then the dopamine faded and the price chugged on as usual and more and more breadcrumbs started spawning with more and more dates. Ended up selling all at around $18 thinking I'd slurp at single digits and finally make it but have yet to buy back, I don't think I have it in me to baghold for more dates desu

62466028
>>62465841
What part of
>not needed
is not getting through to you?

62466034
>>62463670
>No, but 4=4

62466154
Pornlayer peelers shedding at an extreme rate

62466163
i need more linkies

62466164
>>62465917
>ICP
I bet you do, buddy.

62466376
>>62465841
>>62465696
>>62465917
The Swift ledger is built specifically to be connected to many chains, private and public.
The future is multichain, haven't you heard?

You L1 maxis really need to wipe the shit from your eyeballs already.

62466383
>>62466028
what the fuck are you talking about
>>62466376
>built specifically to be connected to many chains, private and public
you only need 1 public chain for ordering, that's it
and since ordering is done there, the liquidity is also there
a private chain is an oxymoron because you don't need to order anything,you're not going to sandwich attack/double spend your own fucking transaction
this is apparently what xrpiglets didn't understand yet concerning this "tech" and are hopelessly illiterate on

62466401
>>62466383
>you only need 1 public chain

L1 maxi detected.
The future is multichain, how are you not wise to this yet holy shit.

62466434
it's all so tiresome
https://x.com/ChrisBarrett/status/1972765646976123278

62467985
>>62463144
Bearish for LINK unironically
>pilot

62467991
>>62466383
>you only need 1 public chain for ordering, that's it
This is impossible.

62467996
>>62467985
>live

cope with it

62468876
>>62463144
Link and swift are both trash and deserve each other.

62469750
>>62463385
Why? It has no impact on the token whatsoever.

62472814
>>62468876
whatever you say, Brad

62475416
>>62467996
Are these live pilots in the room with us right now?

62475514
Looking like the UK is getting its shit together much faster than the US and the clarity act. UK is actively pushing forward fast.

62475615
>>62475416
>s-s-swift is LYING!!!

Not a good week for xerpies

62475698
>>62463144
>Yet crypto is in the shitter, why?
crypto is a scam from the top down

62475871
>>62475698
That's definitely the impression new money has when looking at the top 20.
Which is why they flock to the stock market instead.

62475889
>SWIFT THREAD #4281

item.title: Sell your crapto and pivot to AI, we still have plenty of room to grow: 1. — 36 replies — 23.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Sell your crapto and pivot to AI, we still have plenty of room to grow: 1.

Open thread • Replies: 36 • Updated: 2026-07-13 14:00 UTC

OP

Sell your crapto and pivot to AI, we still have plenty of room to grow:

1. Nebius Group (NBIS) — Hyper-growth Cloud Leader
Q1 2026 revenue: +684% YoY.
2026 projections: ~544–550% revenue growth; 2027 ~225–234%.

2. Sandisk (SNDK) — Memory/Storage Explosive Gainer
YTD stock gains: ~465–857% (top performer in many trackers).

3. Micron Technology (MU) — Memory Powerhouse
YTD gains: ~300–1000%+ in some periods; one-year leader in many AI indices.

4. Broadcom (AVGO): AI semiconductor revenue +143% recently, with Q3 guidance >200% growth. Large-scale with strong margins.

5. Seagate (STX), Western Digital (WDC): Storage plays with 180–550%+ gains.


There's no catalyst for crapto and won't ever be and we both know it.

Early replies (first 3)

62469845
No ma'am, I cannot accept sexual favors as compensation because that's not what we agreed on with the terms. Perhaps we can work out a payment plan? Can you please put your pants back on?

62469846
>>62469805
You want to make money? Make games like pic related

62469852
>>62469846
Saturated. There are villages in my home country that are devoted to making these now. Everyone contributes different aspects to the game.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62469872
>>62469865
/biz/ makes sense when you realize this is just where the Greater Fools hang out

62469880
>>62469805
Are you just listing what already pumped, saying now it's a good time to buy it?

62469887
>>62469865
Many such cases

62469892
>>62469872
>>62469887
>sell your crypto at the bottom
>pay massive taxes
>move the funds into a tradfi stock trading account
>buy the top of AI stocks with half the money you had in crypto because u lost half to taxes
>AI stocks dump 90%
>crypto starts pumping again

62470054
>>62469805
Sauce on the image

62470060
>>62469852
What games are you talking about? renpy milf games?

62470091
>>62470054
there's literally a sauce watermark you retard

62470111
>>62469805
SAUCE NOW

62470121
>>62469805
This is like the equivalent for scammers coming in here trying to convince retards into buy their shitcoin after it already pumped 500+%. No thanks

62470218
>>62470111
>SAUCE NOW
yes.
also, checked

62470245
>>62469805
MY SEMEN INSIDE THIS INK BLOTCH NOW

62470271
>>62470091
>there's literally a sauce watermark you retard
nya nya nyat thEreS LitERLLy a SaUcE WATerMaRK yOu ReTarD nya nya
stfu drok

62470283
>>62469892
>pay massive taxes
you dont pay taxes on losses frend

62470301
>>62470283
Depends where you live

62470322
>>62470271

62470392
Crypto is never going up again. There are no more retards to be conned into buying it. It does nothing. It has no use case.

62470396
>>62469805
I will accept payment in this way at the exchange rate of 1 creampie per eris.

62470402
When the jeets are abandoning crypto scams for FOTM stock bag selling I'd say we're in the bottom for crypto and the top for AI stocks.

62470491
>>62469870

House in the Rift
Desert Stalker
Dreamland
Sisterly Lust
Zombie in my Cabin
One in a Lifetime / Eternum
Realm Invader
Shale Hill Secrets
Space Journey X
Seeds of Chaos
Innocent Withes (grind)

62470495
>>62470491

62470624
>>62470054
>>62470111
>>62470218
>>62470245
>>62470271
>>62470322
https://rule34.xxx/index.php?page=post&s=view&id=16431712

62470663
>>62470402
This.

62473334
>>62470402
>and the top for AI stocks
you dont say

62473846
Is this the hot stock tip thread? I might buy two or three of them after doing some analysis.

62473916
>>62470624
Updoots to you, young squire.

62473938
>>62473334
>stonks can't pump and dump like crypto!

62474031
I think it's cool when people post
>550% gains
Using data from JANUARY when it's JULY
So basically it's like what, 10% more gains to next year? Wow thanks bro.

62474094
>>62469805
SEXOOOOOOOOOOOOO!!!! *ahem* this reward is unacceptable. I shall accept either the full reward in gold within 12 hours or you may pay me with your hand in marriage.
>and just like that I have one sexy wife

With that out of the way, shill me on Nebius. I don't really get what they make/do at all and why you have them ranked so highly.

I understand Sandisk and Micron, but what about Broadcom, Seagate, and Western Digital. What is their play about?

And finally what our your price targets for these? A lot of them have pumped a lot, I don't want to buy in and 2 months later they're -50% or worse. Like you know why don't you shill me this before it already pumped. You don't exactly give off genius trader vibes when you speak from a position that only comes after the fact. You'd need proof of having been in the trade prior to the pump.

62474480
>>62469845
>Can you please put your pants back on?

H...Hold on, before you do.... ***SSNIIIFFFFF*** No no, I was right, see this wont do at all. *SNIFFFFF AGAIN TO MAKE SURE.* Nope, not at all.

62475676
>>62473938
nvidia hasnt pumped yet thou
ayyymd is surreal, they arent even an ai stock

item.title: >mfw it's virtually impossible to make more than 100k+ a year if you aren't a US citizen The world economy is literally… — 23 replies — 10.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
>mfw it's virtually impossible to make more than 100k+ a year if you aren't a US citizen The world economy is literally…

Open thread • Replies: 23 • Updated: 2026-07-13 13:23 UTC

OP

>mfw it's virtually impossible to make more than 100k+ a year if you aren't a US citizen

The world economy is literally rigged

Early replies (first 3)

62470620
most US citizens don't make that much either

62470627
>>62470461
Come to America. Join us. Vote Republican. Embrace classical liberal Americana. Hate everything communist and fascist. You will thrive.

62470632
>>62470627
ok give me a visa
i would leave europe tomorrow

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62470641
>>62470632
Actual American here. Sorry we’re full unfortunately!

62470644
>>62470632
Only jeets allowed

62470648
>>62470632
We'll trade you for 10 Haitians.

>>62470641
No, we need to trade. Let the loving communist Europeans take our "refugees".

62470923
>>62470620
Yes, but I am highly qualified and I make 15k a year in a third world country. If I were american i would earn at least 90k a year

62471418
>>62470461
Half of that 100k, if you even make that much (few do), goes towards healthcare.

62471423
>>62470461
pretty sure we got illegal immigrants working construction making that. The secret is to be in the US, not necessarily to be a citizen

62471442
>>62470632
>public transit, subsidized healthcare, labor and consumer protections, social safety net, basic human rights that still carry weight, and mostly functional democracies
I'll trade you. Take my US passport and I'll take yours to live my life out in the EU.

62472269
>>62471442
lmao
healthcare is literally more expensive here unless you earn less than €24k or so ie you might as well not even work

62472277
>>62471442
If you actually believe Europe is better than the US, then GTFO of our country, instead of trying to destroy it with your socialist faggotry.

62472559
>>62470461
Jack ma did it

62473195
>>62470461
$100k is like 70th percentile here so it's not that common here either

62473217
>>62472277
No comrade, the whole world be turn to communism and worship niggers!

62473219
>>62473217
Nooooooooo!!!!!

62473247
>>62470461
Not really, im a europoor and in my sector its entirely possible to achieve 100k+

You know how much that is after taxes though? 58k

62473356
>>62472269
What the hell country are you from where healthcare is more expensive than the US? Every article comparing costs that I could find says they pay more than anyone else in the world, by a lot, and for questionable service even. Sure you might prepay in taxes, but even that is less than Amerifats pay for insurance, not even counting what they pay the hospital after insurance.

62473366
>>62471418
Lmao this is such cope, anyone in a decent non retail job has their healthcare covered, my job fully covers healthcare expenses over $3k per year, which I have never hit but it’s nice to have, I definitely pay way less than I would if I had to pay for it in taxes.

62474451
>>62473356
talk to a european you know isn't a total loser neet about what they pay in deductions and compare it to what you pay
i barely walk away with half my paycheck and over 10% of it goes to healthcare
do you or any american you know pay 10% of your Income to healthcare? i have never met one who does
even ones paying for personal cover only pay ~$400 a month and the premium is rarely relevant but even at $2000 it wouldn't be 10%
>and for questionable service even
american healthcare is some of the best in the world after countries like switzerland, japan, and norway
moreover they get something
i do not get any screenings or any kind of annual check up until i'm 45 minimum, unless i have a seizure etc
that is really what i hate, i get nothing beyond vague promises which wont come true because preventative medicine > curative medicine
it's like social security, the young are just paying for the old but it will never be reciprocated

62474728
>>62470461
I make $118k ($73k after taxes) a year working as a software engineer, non-US. I live with my parents and save/invest almost $5k a month. I also have a company match and I have a shitton of money in my retirement fund.
I must say I'm from one of the few countries where a software engineer still good money.
>>62471423
No they don't.

62474932
>>62470461
I make $156k CAD, but in a few more months that will be less than $100k USD so you're probably right.

62475517
>>62470461
>it's virtually impossible to make more than 100k+ a year if you aren't a US citizen
Start your own company. You can make as much as you want, if it's successful.

OP

My social contract is BROKEN. I live only out of the simple drive to maintain homeostasis. Nothing in my life matters anymore.
Therefore i simply will not pay.

Early replies (first 3)

62475412
>>62475361
This has nothing to do with business and/or finance.

62476020
>>62475412
It has everything to do with business and finance.
The economy is overly oppressive to those with low sexual market value

item.title: Have any UK anons faked a job reference before? — 9 replies — 3.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Have any UK anons faked a job reference before?

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-07-13 13:05 UTC

OP

Have any UK anons faked a job reference before? How did you do it? Should I ask family or friends to pretend to be a former employer? Should I make up a fake business that recently shut down? Will any jobs I apply to accept gmail or hotmail emails as job references?

I was thinking of making up a window cleaning company, a small company of 4 or 5 employees that recently shut down, will reference checkers be suspicious that such a company doesn't have an online presence and can I just claim that we recently shut down and closed down our website as an excuse?

Early replies (first 3)

62471772
>>62470742
Tell them to call me, I will them you're a proper geezer

62471810
just start butchering niggers on the street en masse

62472268
bump

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62472272
Yes. What are they going to do? Chase up old employees of closed down businesses on social media?
You were regional sales manager for Toys R Us, congrats.

62472291
>>62470742
>I was thinking of making up a window cleaning company, a small company of 4 or 5 employees that recently shut down
why are you 'making up' a co?
just use one that went provably titsup at approx the right period, maybe not one as obvious as >>62472272. Try not to use 2 or more bustcos as references - you'll start to look unlucky - and some Doctor Who(ever) as another personal reference, and setup a gmail (DrBlah@gmail.com) in case someone actually contacts this, which they won't. Unless HR are cunts, in which case, you better off out of it.
I dunno what you are applying for, but I'm not aware of many big cos still even asking for references, never mind checking the fucking things. But have a backstory ready just in case they ask.

62472303
>>62472291
I'm applying to entry level customer service/Admin Jobs, minimum wage salary for the UK ~£25,000. These types of Jobs still need references which is retarded.

I'm worried if I use a real company that shut down they'll still have an online presence, former employees who'll be contacted and they'll say that I never worked there. What I was thinking is to give the email of one of my brothers as a reference for the company I made up, and for the interview I'll train really well to answer questions related to the fake company I made up and the role that I supposedly did there.

62472316
>>62472303
>Customer Service
I'd find a plausibly local call centre that went titsup or got taken over, rndm example:
https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cr5804d8l6po
figure out what the format of their co. emails would be - myoldboss@totallytitsupnow.co, you gold. No-one checks this shit ever, not if it's a company name email, and worst case scenario, it just bounces.

62472440
>>62470742
If you're putting a window cleaning company on your CV then the job you're applying for doesn't care about references at all

62475473
bump

item.title: Japan is supporting bankers to invest in the USA — 3 replies — 3.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Japan is supporting bankers to invest in the USA

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-07-13 14:33 UTC

OP

Bull signal. Buy Nikkei and S&P.
>The Japanese government is considering a framework to help major banks secure U.S. dollar funding to finance investment projects under Tokyo's $550 billion deal struck with the United States last year in exchange for tariff relief, a source close to the matter said Friday.
>Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's government is considering a request for support from Japan's three biggest banks -- MUFG Bank, Sumitomo Mitsui Banking Corp. and Mizuho Bank -- which are concerned about the cost of securing dollar funding.
>"It is impossible to secure dollars on our own to finance all the U.S. projects under the (trade) agreement," a megabank source said.
https://mainichi.jp/english/articles/20260711/p2g/00m/0bu/006000c

Early replies (first 3)

62474602
>>62474593
the japanese economy and yen is gonna crash

62475446
>>62474593
Americans are trying to reverse the money flowing from America to japan right now, it's the plaza accords 2.0 because Americans forced the japanese to invest into american stocks but it's not profitable anymore because yen interest rates are going up which is making money go back to japan. The yen carry trade ended.

62475833
>>62474593
>let's fuck our economy and people while Corporations and rich fags are going to make a lot money
i am so fucking tired of this shithole World

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

62470823

62470829

amazing it still hasnt fallen 50% oh wait thats' because 4% of the total supply is available. my mistake.

62470830
still above the ipo price. not bad.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62471760
If this goes down, there will be literal riots to bail out bagholders. So much retail money is riding Elon's rocket.

62471765
>>62471760
>literal riots
r/wallstreetbets won't do shit

62472184
>>62471090
>SPCX is a good business (Starlink)
Starlink is a cool idea, in the relative Musk Universe of Flying (& Exploding) Horseshit. But even then, you start scratching the surface and it falls apart. It 'makes money' because the fraudulent fat fuck needed at least one co in SPCX portfolio of space garbage that looked like it did. irl, its launch costs are hugely subsidized, those sats have a relatively short shelf life and obv. servicing logistic problematic, system gets worse more customers it has and would fall over at scale, yadda. Its an expensive argument against gravity. And this supposedly SPCX golden child cash cow.

62472192
>>62471760
hope they are mowed down by the national guard, the average iq would improve a lot

62472195
>>62472184
Starlink is also very niche when you think about it. We already have 5G coverage in most of the civilized world. Where it is used, can be considered niche areas like for example Ukrainian drones, airplanes, cruise ships and poor rural places that dont have internet today. How much of a market is that really?

62472196
ATH was $200. Think it'll return to that level in our lifetime?

62472199
>>62472196
LOL no

or may be in 2030

62472271
>>62470796
I'm still baffled by how retarded 90% of /biz/ is. Then I remember most of the oldfag White /biz/ users are gone and this place is 90% jeets. Give it 30 years and MAYBE some of you retards will start to grasp basic economics and financial markets. It really is like your brains are incapable of logic based thinking, even when you've been exposed to the correct information for over a decade. Instead you keep posting the same threads over and over again, thinking you're disseminating some kind of profound knowledge.

62472285
>>62470916
No refunds h*manoyim

62472320
>>62472271
thats a lot of words that say absolutely nothing

62472376
>>62471124
I'll buy when i can have a grok-powered harem of cute catgirls (some catboys too) waifus sucking my cock and doing my house chores 24/7

62472383
>>62472184
>its launch costs are hugely subsidized
>t. literal retard
SpaceX prices its launches far below any other launch provider. They've taken over the entire industry, aside from Chinese/Russian military shit, the Eurotards trying to remain relevant with their three Ariane launches per year, and a few very small players that are desperately trying to horn in (like Blew-Up Origin and Rocket Lab).

Once Super Heavy survives testing and fixing, launch costs are expected to drop by well over another order of magnitude -- some are saying $200/kg to orbit, which means that even ur mom could afford to go (assuming they could cram her inside the fairing).

62472392
>>62472383
>blabla
>entirely ignores actual fucking point he is claiming to respond to
>Moar! Musk PR waffle
>unfunny your Mom 'joke'
isn't it time for your k-doze, fatboi?

62472429
>>62470796
When is the next big unlock? I want to buy puts.

62472445
>>62472320
Yes, if you have an IQ below the White average, you'll struggle with understanding what I just texted. Thanks for making my point.

62472495
I said this shit was going to 135 from the beginning and you baggies called me retarded. Imagine not selling in the first 2 or 3 days after the IPO kek

62472544
>>62470796
>-4%
kek do stocktards really?

62472735
>>62472544
Yeah, that's the kind of daily volatility people expect from a Musk company

62472968
>>62470796
I planned to buy on release and sell the first bump up but didn't have cash available at the time. Shame. I played another meme IPO the exact same way in the past as I was just entering the stock market and it had a very similar pattern.

62474064
>>62472445
no one in this entire world understands you. you are alone.

62475040
>>62470796
waiting for a year after IPO to buy in because Im not stupid and know that we are still in the early adoption phase in terms of what the company is capable of.

62475043
>>62470796
When the spinning ball-Earth is finally exposed worldwide for the 400+ year deception it was, Earth's entire population will suddenly be faced with the reality that every government, every space agency, university, secret society, religious organization, mainstream and alternative media outlet have ALL been duplicitous in propping up a monstrous manipulation to fleece and control the masses. The resulting mass mental exodus away from the control system is exactly what humanity needs. Once the flat Earth truth gets out, these lying politicians, spokesmen, reporters and teachers suddenly change from being heralded voices of authority to being ridiculed, shunned and denounced as they deserve. Once the flat Earth truth gets out, these governments, universities, media outlets and other entangled organizations which have long been hard at work weaving this multi-generational ball-Earth myth, suddenly and completely lose all credibility. Once the truth of our flat Earth gets out, so does the truth of these few elite families/societies who have kept this most important and fundamental reality from us for these hundreds of years! Essentially, once the flat Earth truth gets out, so does every other important truth by proxy, because this "mother-of-all-conspiracies" holds under its umbrella literally ALL of the other conspiracies, and exposes them.

62475117
>>62470796
wheres the worst possible place you can put a rouge ai?
>where you cant fucking unplug it, it has millions of ways to connect to everything on earth that is electronic and distributed over wide area all over our planet.
this is profitable.

62475330
just wait until shares start to unlock, the price is going to fucking tank

62475669
still about $135 more valuable than it should be

62475697
>>62470830

62475706
>>62470796
>tons of people own stock prior to IPO
>no significant new developments to warrant price increases

item.title: Has Bitcoin become too dependent on ETFs and macro news? — 103 replies — 35.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Has Bitcoin become too dependent on ETFs and macro news?

Open thread • Replies: 103 • Updated: 2026-07-13 12:40 UTC

OP

Seems like every BTC pump or dump is blamed on ETF flows or macro headlines.

>Is that just the reality now, or are people overlooking other factors?

Early replies (first 3)

62450753
>>62450728
Its 100% cz dependent, just tradingview has to write something to the price action.

62450760
>>62450728
Btc does this thing where it lulls the newfag into a false sense of superiority. Both in bull and bear markets. In a bull market, it tricks newfags into thinking it will go up forever and traps them in overleveraged longs and in bear markets, it tricks them into it never going back up and they either sell low, sell covered calls or lever up short and get milked. Just hold spot and don't pay attention for a decade.

62450902
>>62450753
cz?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62464142
>>62464098
You're too invested in that drama. It's not happening in real life.

62464251
>>62464121
You know what? That's fair. I relied too much on 90s sitcom tropes. It should just end episodically, there's just no more seasons and that's it.
>>62464142
I don't have much going on in my life and my family and friends find my occurrences boring. And it's supposed to be a comedy. What drama? I want it to be goofy and for all publics!

62464760
>>62464033
Take stablecoins for another example. Zero. Zilch. Nada. $0.00 -- Remember when 100% of USDT used to be on Bitcoin? Now it's 0%, Omni is dead and Tether has moved to every other chain. There is nothing more to Bitcoin than BTC the asset.

62464820
I have a credit card that gives me cash back in the form of BTC. So my investment strategy is just skimming off my purchases anyway.

62465005
>>62464251
Why do you think you're right about that and you think you're so sure about it?

62465089
>>62465005
I don't understand the question. Right about what.

62466102
>>62464115
Cool. There's clearly more happening around bitcoin now than there was a few years ago.

62466109
>>62464760
I'm talking about native BTC, not stablecoins. Those are two different discussions.

62466120
Imagine having to pay for each website you visited…that’s how stupid it is for proof of work, btc will never work long term (already sucks now)

62466195
>>62459656
Well this is a link board so I won't say anything negative. I hope link goes to 10k eoy for all the anons holding it.

62466244
>>62466102
You do realize Aave is on Ethereum, right? It's great for BTC the asset but not so great for Bitcoin the network. When you're saying innovation isn't happening on Bitcoin base layer but "around it", do you mean on Aave for example?
>>62466109
And I was talking about innovation on Bitcoin in general. Originally I replied to
>why are people still building new infrastructure around it?
saying there's no infrastructure, well... besides bridging BTC to Ethereum. Saying there's no stablecoin infra on top of Bitcoin is relevant to the discussion.

62466717
>>62466244
Bridging is risky

62466757
>>62466195
Chinlink

62467179
>>62466120
What drugs you're taking ser?

62467263
>>62466195
buy link

62467380
>>62463301
Is this real

62468734
>>62463320
I remember this .how time flies

62469658
>Icp

62469661
>>62464820
How's that possible?

62470315
>>62469658
I don't know any reason to sell

62471740
>>62463265
Whales = me

62472334
>>62463285
No way. You don't understand what Bitcoin is

62472669
>>62466717
Why would you risk it

62472729
>>62450728
is this the COME AND FILE YO TAXES guy?

62473419
>>62472729
be clear anon

62473816
>>62469661
Coinbase has a card that does it.

62473917
Will Saylor sponsor a team, in Miami maybe?

62475220
>>62473816
Don't trust

62475407
>>62475220
Don't trust what? Coinbase? Just transfer your coin to whatever other wallet you have if you don't trust it.

item.title: Sell SPCX you fools. Sell until it's too late. — 127 replies — 22.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Sell SPCX you fools. Sell until it's too late.

Open thread • Replies: 127 • Updated: 2026-07-13 09:55 UTC

OP

Sell SPCX you fools. Sell until it's too late.

Early replies (first 3)

62466535
They should just make corporate espionage legal in western countries at this point. The only people who are winning from the current laws are the Chinese

62466548
>>62466527
>state media says
lol

62466549
>>62466546
Show me one innovation from Japan.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62469347
>>62466610
It's a very interesting story, but a part that is easy to forget is that he had an investor fully bankrolling his efforts for multiple years with $3 millions.

62469665
this is some shit that a university student should be able to do as a minimum requirement. wtf is so complicated?

62469774
>>62468335
>Although /sp/'s average IQ level is (maybe?) below this board's average,
Seriously?? How many people here bought Chainlink or Safemoon? Not even mentioning all the Indian shitcoin scams.

62469777
>>62469347
>a part that is easy to forget is that he had an investor fully bankrolling his efforts for multiple years with $3 millions.
So what? How is that even relevant to the point that it was a Japanese scientist who invented the blue LED?

And the "investor" was the Japanese shit-tier semiconductor company he worked for, whose Japanese CEO saw the business deteriorating and threw the money at the Japanese scientist as a "hail Mary" attempt to create a product that would save the Japanese company.

"OMG he had an investor!!!" So fucking what?

62469825
>>62469777
>So fucking what?
None of this would happen without the $3 million investment. None of this. The guy was extremely lucky in this regard that he had such an opportunity.

62469856
it's using a net lmao

62469886
>>62466527
Did China steal blueprints from SpaceX?

62470354
>>62469886
Long March 10B is about as different from Falcon 9 as a rocket can get while still being a rocket that does propulsive landing. Cable catch, dual fuel, seven engined booster, closed cycle engines, oxidizer-rich preburner, etc.

62470360
>>62466535
Corporate espionage happens all the time regardless of its legality and there's zero chance that the Chinks aren't just being used by Western alphabet agencies. The moment they come up with, test, build or create anything that is a legitimate threat you can expect everyone involved to suicide by two shots to the back of the head or for the entire complex to have a rocket accident and get blown up in the explosion. People do not share power, especially not over international borders.

>>62466548
Also this. You can generally assume that everything China ever says is just lies projected to a face-saving culture. Its for domestic consumption, not for us to speculate about.

62470370
Buy SPCX, the prices won’t get much lower than this. You don’t want to be an earth bagholder, do you?

62470418
>>62470360
>The moment they come up with, test, build or create anything that is a legitimate threat you can expect everyone involved to suicide by two shots to the back of the head or for the entire complex to have a rocket accident and get blown up in the explosion

They should have done that before Chinese satellites ruined Trump's Iran adventure by providing Iran with the exact locations in real time of air defenses, aircraft parking spots and other assets in the region, as well as providing damage assessment so Iran didn't waste missiles on an already destroyed target

62470662
>>62466603
>The one really great thing about this is that maybe now China will stop crashing boosters onto their own citizens' villages.
It's due to the fact their spaceport is so far inland it's impossible to not drop on it's citizens. Russia's fall over Siberia, while USA shoots over oceans.

62471032
>>62466603
>>62470662
Rockets launched from Wenchang fall into the sea, not onto populated areas. It's the rockets launched from Xichang that fall onto populated areas. This is because the location for Xichang spaceport was chosen during the Cold War when security from military strike was considered very important. There are no unpopulated in the Chinese interior at the low latitudes from which you can launch to geosynchronous orbit. Xichang spaceport will probably be closed within 5 years, or at most 10 years.

62471086
>>62470662
only an issue because of hypergolic fuels
otherwise its just a random debris cloud

62471098
>>62466590
>regional conflict in eastern yurop
we're talking about big picture stuff here, anon. no one cares about the vatnik-gopnik war

62471197
>>62468297
>>62468335
>noo you HAVE to write in correct english and use common english saying correctly for us to trust you!
your country is owned by jews who speak your language only because it's convenient to them. the universal language of the future will be chinese, while the US will be speaking spanish in the next decades, so get used to it, RETARD.

62471338
>>62467458
>>62467520
so starship isn't a factor? the servers in space thing is not priced in?

62471395
>>62466527
Kek, of course it looks just like Elon's. If China didnt have the US to steal from theys be an absolute disaster failure state.

62471694
>>62471338
>the servers in space thing
Complete nonsense, zero chance of turning a profit, ever, without miraculous breakthroughs in materials science. I work as a cloud engineer in the space industry, I mange enormous numbers of servers that are used by an entire subdivision of our company that are employed for the sole task of determining if a satellite's design is going cause it to experience runaway heat issues and fail. The idea of putting racks of fucking GPUs of all things into LEO is what we would come up with if we wanted to create a parody of what a clueless pleb would try to pitch to investors. Its going to be another full self-driving mode, robotaxi, robovan (remember that one?), humanoid robots, and the mission to mars. Its a fantasy, its not happening. He'll probably try, no doubt, and it will work for a month or so, stock will pump massively, and then fail.

62471784
>>62469825
faggot, do you think nobody else would have invented the blue led?

62471789
>>62466535
what makes you think the US havent already been doing this for decades at this point in supposedly 'allied' western countries like japan and s korea? long before the chinese ever became a serious threat

62471902
>>62471694
>zero chance of turning a profit
anon, we are not talking about reality or about what YOU think about it. and investors think "oh, they made a reusable rocket, they surely have enough engineering knowledge or ingenuity to pull it off!", so people invest based on assumptions and fantasies rather than on reality. just look at the AI bubble...

62471927
>>62471784
He wouldn't. He wouldn't invent anything without the funding. You seem to be very angry about this.

62471932
>>62471927
who cares who does it, why do you think it matters and why do you think i care about that one guy?

62471938
>>62471932
Try to read the posts you're replying to you fucking retard

62471973
>>62471694
>turning a profit
why would you think this is important, bigger companies have survived for longer without profit, your small minded thinking does not work at this scale

62472725
>>62471395
>muh it's also a cylinder so it has to be a carbon copy
Long March 10B is about as different from Falcon 9 as a rocket can be. Different engine count, different propellants, different engine combustion cycle, different preburner, different recovery method.

62472738
>>62466624
>(Elon's never going to sell his half of the company.)
The fat arsehole has preferential stock.
You'd still need to suck his dick if you bought 80% of this fucking garbage.

62474107
spcx is gonna go down 20% at open because of this stupid FUD

62475125
spcx is going to 30 because they are about to float 400x the amount of shares lmao
u guys are complete retards
